title: Center on U.S.–China Relations
gist: A landing page of Asia Society Center on U.S.-China Relations research surfacing recurring investor-relevant themes: China's rare-earths leverage and U.S. de-risking, semiconductor concentration in Taiwan, the argument that war over Taiwan is avoidable, and the diplomatic cadence around a 2026 Trump-Xi summit.

insights.claim: A Center paper by Sam George argues China deliberately built its rare-earths leverage while the U.S. system failed to mobilize, and it assesses the Trump administration's latest moves to de-risk the U.S. minerals supply chain.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: Frames rare earths as a structural Chinese chokepoint and U.S. de-risking as an active policy driver — watch for supply-chain reshoring beneficiaries.

insights.claim: A joint Asia Society–Hoover Institution report ('Silicon Triangle') notes that most of the world's semiconductor supply comes from Taiwan and recommends how the U.S. and allies can ensure a reliable chip supply.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: Reiterates the single-point-of-failure concentration of chip supply in Taiwan, the core geopolitical risk underpinning fab diversification bets.

themes.theme: Taiwan war risk and cross-strait dialogue

insights.claim: A Task Force on U.S.-China Policy brief argues that despite rising China-Taiwan-U.S. tensions, avoiding war in the Taiwan Strait is both essential and possible.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: A measured counter to escalation narratives; useful base-case framing for pricing Taiwan-conflict tail risk.

insights.claim: KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun publicly discussed prospects for dialogue with Beijing and whether a stable cross-strait status quo can be maintained amid rising tensions.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: KMT leadership signaling openness to Beijing dialogue is a marginal de-escalation indicator for cross-strait risk.

insights.claim: Asia Society convened experts to examine a 2026 Trump-Xi leaders' summit, focusing on strategic priorities and diplomatic dynamics for U.S.-China relations.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: A 2026 Trump-Xi summit is a scheduled catalyst worth watching for shifts in tariff, tech, and minerals policy.

insights.claim: A Task Force working group report co-published with the German Council on Foreign Relations maps converging and diverging approaches to China among traditional U.S. allied countries.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: Allied alignment on China is uneven; divergence among allies is a constraint on coordinated economic pressure.
insights.locator: Allies and Partners: The Future of Relations with China

insights.claim: A Task Force report argues the U.S. should prioritize Southeast Asia in its China policy, laying out challenges and recommended government actions.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: Flags Southeast Asia as the contested arena where U.S.-China competition is underweighted in policy.
insights.locator: Prioritizing Southeast Asia in American China Policy
